projectTypeOverlay: Private hosted single-owner accounting service moving to a dedicated Ubuntu VM on Newsec with immutable release/image authority, root-owned deployment and cutover receipts, systemd-owned lifecycle, Tailscale-only ingress, and verified recovery units.
purpose: Private, single-owner EasyFire bookkeeping web application, based on Bigcapital (AGPL-3.0), for recording and reporting financial activity with durable self-hosted storage and recoverable operations.

multiAgent.upgradeProcedure: Run the creating-project-foundations skill from Command Center and request multi-agent mode; do not run a project-local npm command that is not installed here.

deploymentContext.audience: One owner and administrator on the private Tailscale tailnet with native application authentication and public self-registration disabled. No public website route is part of the superseding endpoint.
deploymentContext.networkExposure: The target exposes exactly one tailnet-only Tailscale Serve HTTPS host, easyfire-bookkeeping-newsec.taild63e9b.ts.net:443, proxying to Envoy at 127.0.0.1:8080. Tailscale Funnel, public host listeners, Cloudflare cutover, and direct database/cache exposure are forbidden. The old Windows Cloudflare route remains live and unchanged only until gated cutover.
deploymentContext.trustedComputeBoundary: Newsec is the owner-controlled Hyper-V host. The production target is the isolated Ubuntu 24.04 VM easyfire-bookkeeping-newsec with Docker Engine, Compose, systemd, and Tailscale; a separate isolated VM owns destructive/reboot rehearsal. Windows Docker data, volumes, releases, journals, backups, tasks, and edge remain preserved rollback authority until cutover completes.
deploymentContext.availabilityTarget: Best-effort private production service. All Compose restart policies are no; easyfire-bookkeeping-stack.service is the sole boot/start authority after exact fixed-plan verification. The systemd Guardian may recover exact stateless services only and observes MariaDB/Redis without restarting or mutating them. Maintenance downtime is acceptable; high availability is not required.
deploymentContext.assuranceTarget: Single-owner native authentication; private Tailscale-only ingress; immutable release, OCI and engine image identity; schema-2 checkpoint transfer authority; exact deployment and cutover plans/receipts; isolated restore; separate rehearsal VM; single-writer, rollback, reboot, and Guardian recovery proof; and preservation of every Windows runtime artifact until gated closeout.

code.remoteUrl: private Forgejo repository jonny-admin/easyfire-bookkeeping (machine-specific endpoint is intentionally not published)
code.recoveryPath: Exact Forgejo main readback defines accepted source and anonymous GitHub readback of the same commit defines AGPL corresponding source. The verified pre-takeover filesystem backup remains the local rollback authority recorded in HANDOFF.md.

sourceOfTruth.runningRuntime: On 2026-07-23, the original Windows Bookkeeping runtime on Newsec remains the sole live production writer and rollback authority; all original volumes, releases, journals, backups, tasks, and the Cloudflare route remain preserved. The Linux production VM easyfire-bookkeeping-newsec remains off and has received no production traffic. The isolated v6 rehearsal VM has the pinned application stack safely stabilized active with six healthy long-running containers and loopback HTTP 200. Fresh backup 20260723T075310Z plus isolated restore, rollback lock/rearm, and Guardian proof passed. Runtime recovery failed twice because the sealed release raced Docker health=starting; no recovery proof, normal-reboot marker, final rehearsal receipt, native-auth proof, private route, or cutover exists.
sourceOfTruth.driftCheck: Do not rerun runtime recovery or create another compatibility continuation against the current sealed candidate. Commit and independently review the validated six-file manifest-mode/transient-health repair, build a new immutable release, and rerun only proof invalidated by that changed release, followed by one bounded recovery-only rehearsal. Continue to normal reboot, native authentication, final rehearsal collection, production staging, source quiescence, final checkpoint, single-writer proof, and tailnet-only route activation only if every preceding gate passes. Preserve Windows as the sole writer and keep the production VM off until then.

sourceControlPolicy.localCommit: Allowed after validation for durable work when the requested lane is complete.
sourceControlPolicy.createRemote: Requires explicit approval; scaffolding may record a planned URL but does not create or configure it.
sourceControlPolicy.push: Requires explicit approval or a standing project-specific instruction that clearly authorizes publication.
sourceControlPolicy.merge: Requires explicit approval or the project's documented guarded integration workflow.
sourceControlPolicy.forcePush: Prohibited unless the owner approves the exact branch and impact.

guardrails.impactMap: Docs and disclosure: release-readiness and project-foundation checks. Application or dependency changes: frozen install, format, lint, typecheck, build, focused tests, and fake-data E2E. Linux production/Compose/systemd/Guardian changes: Node or shell parse, focused checkpoint/release/deployment/cutover/rollback/private-route tests, disposable isolated Docker, backup/restore, reboot, and separate-VM rehearsal proof. Runtime changes: fixed-plan verify-existing plus exact receipt/journal/systemd/private-route readback.

sourceSizePolicy.defaultRule: Warnings are advisory. When a finding blocks under the declared control profile, split it or record an exact path-scoped policy threshold plus the path, lines and severity, disproportionate-split reason, containment proof, and a re-review trigger.

guardrails.contractRegistry: TypeScript application contracts plus restart:no Compose, immutable release-manifest v2 and engine evidence, schema-2 Windows checkpoint transfer authority, root-owned Linux deployment plan and receipts, once-only migration/start authority, runtime manifest, systemd sole-start gate, Guardian stateless-only policy, append-only backup/restore evidence, rollback lock/rearm receipts, exclusive source-quiesce and cutover authority, and tailnet-only private-route activation receipt.
guardrails.replayHarness: Synthetic E2E data and a disposable isolated Docker Compose project are required before live production acceptance.
guardrails.stateMutationApproval: Explicit approval is required for deploy, restart, rollback, migration, restore, systemd/Tailscale changes, credentials, real financial record mutation, and Git publication or integration. The approved direct-to-VM scope authorizes only the isolated Linux candidate and exact cutover path after backup, rollback, authentication, separate-rehearsal, reboot, recovery, final-checkpoint, and single-writer proofs pass. Cloudflare and the Windows runtime remain preserve-only.

securityAndPrivacy.securityRequirements: The target requires native JWT/application authentication, SIGNUP_DISABLED=true, empty signup allowlists, secrets outside Git, exact release/controller/image/systemd/runtime identity, verified backup/restore, and exactly one private Tailscale Serve host. Funnel, public listeners, direct MariaDB/Redis exposure, and Cloudflare cutover are prohibited. Owner login may pause only for password, MFA, or CAPTCHA entry.
securityAndPrivacy.sensitiveData: Real financial records, identity and contact information, attachments, database credentials, JWT and SMTP secrets, and optional provider credentials may exist only in production-owned storage and secret boundaries.
securityAndPrivacy.dependencyRiskCurrentEvidence: The complete production audit on the release candidate reports 45 advisories: 9 low, 36 moderate, 0 high, and 0 critical. Build-only tools are excluded from production dependencies, patched runtime transitives are lock-enforced, and remaining moderate/low findings stay disclosed for future refreshes.
securityAndPrivacy.remoteSecurity: Exact Forgejo readback defines private accepted-source authority and anonymous GitHub readback of the same commit defines the public AGPL mirror. Secrets, databases, backups, journals, credentials, and user data must never be published.

reproducibility.runtimeVersion: Node.js 22.23.1 from .nvmrc and pnpm 10.34.5 from packageManager; Docker images use immutable release-specific tags or digests.
reproducibility.dependencyManifest: package.json files across the pnpm workspace, pnpm-workspace.yaml, pnpm-lock.yaml, and docker-compose.prod.yml
reproducibility.lockfilePolicy: Commit pnpm-lock.yaml and reject release installs that do not use --frozen-lockfile.

validation.defaultCommand: node --test tests/easyfire-release-readiness.test.mjs && powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/production/validate.ps1 && node scripts/project-foundation-check.mjs && node scripts/source-size-guard.mjs --changed
validation.impactRule: Use the impact map to choose focused checks before broad checks.

runtimeTruth.source: Use sourceOfTruth.runningRuntime for the real running surface. Windows remains live authority until the exclusive cutover chain completes. The Linux VM gains production authority only through the hash-bound deployment/cutover receipts, fixed-plan verify-existing proof, systemd and reboot proof, single-writer evidence, native authentication, and private Tailscale route activation receipt.

ignored: generated build, test, coverage, and restore artifacts
artifactPolicy.committedFixtures: Only safe, minimal fixtures needed for tests.
artifactPolicy.retention: Keep runtime journals and receipts off Git as deployment authority. Preserve the complete Windows runtime, volumes, releases, journals, tasks, backups, and edge through migration and rollback closeout. Preserve Linux failed-proof resources and immutable releases for diagnosis. Retain each backup with its dump, Redis payload when applicable, hashes, authority metadata, and isolated-restore proof. Summarize or rotate bulky diagnostics without deleting authority.
